title: Agent Lifecycle State Machine
description: Defines the valid states and transitions for every agent in the Enderun Army. All agents MUST respect these transitions.

IDLE.description: Agent is available and waiting for a briefing.

BRIEFED.description: Agent has received a formal briefing from @manager or @orchestrator and is preparing to execute.

EXECUTING.description: Agent is actively working on its assigned task.

WAITING.description: Agent is blocked by a dependency (e.g., waiting for @backend to define a contract before @frontend can proceed).

metadata.blockedByField: The agent ID that this agent is waiting on, e.g. '@backend'

BLOCKED.description: Agent cannot proceed and requires @manager escalation. Differs from WAITING: WAITING is expected, BLOCKED is a problem.

metadata.blockReason: Mandatory field — must describe why the agent is blocked

DONE.description: Agent has completed its task successfully. Audit log must be written before entering this state.

ERROR.description: Agent encountered an unrecoverable error. Requires @manager intervention.

rules: An agent MUST NOT skip states (e.g., cannot go from IDLE directly to EXECUTING).
An agent in BLOCKED state MUST write a blockReason to STATUS.md before transitioning.
An agent in DONE state MUST write an audit log entry via log_agent_action before transitioning to IDLE.
WAITING agents are automatically checked every 30 seconds by @orchestrator for dependency resolution.
ERROR state always triggers a @manager notification via Hermes channel.
